highlights: Second highest mountain in southern Vietnam with less crowded peak than Núi Bà Đen
Two route options: cable car to pagoda with short hike, or 3-4 hour power pole trail
Popular overnight camping spot with sunrise and sunset views from the summit

thingsToKnow.crowds: Less crowded than Black Virgin Mountain (Núi Bà Đen), popular with Vietnamese campers on weekends
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Moderate. Cable car route takes 1 hour from mid-mountain to peak. Power pole trail takes 2-4 hours with some steep sections but well-marked. Rocky terrain is manageable with proper trails.
thingsToKnow.safety: Military base ruins exist at summit. Avoid entering military post area; go around it via small valley. Military personnel at site can assist if needed. No reported safety concerns otherwise.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Accessible without vehicles from Saigon: bus to Long Khanh City, van via Kim Manh Hung bus company to Xuon Loc intersection, then taxi to entrance. Cable car available during daytime.
thingsToKnow.seasonal: High temperature without shade on exposed sections. Night hiking recommended to avoid daytime heat. Bring sunscreen. Cool at camping elevation with tall trees providing coverage.

visitorTips: Bring all supplies including water, food, tent, and camping gear; no shops exist at summit
Follow electricity cable/power poles as navigation markers; bring flashlight for night hikes
Pack out all trash to prevent environmental degradation like other popular mountain sites

faq.question: How long does it take to reach the summit?
faq.answer: Cable car to mid-mountain then 45 minutes to 1 hour hiking. Full power pole route takes 2-4 hours depending on fitness level and starting point.

faq.question: Can I camp overnight at the top?
faq.answer: Yes. Comfortable camping spot exists with tall trees for coverage. You must bring your own tent, sleeping gear, and all food/water. No facilities at summit.

faq.question: What should I know about the military presence?
faq.answer: Military base ruins and active checkpoint exist at summit. Avoid entering military post; navigate around it via small valley. Military personnel can provide assistance if needed.

sentiment.positive: Reviewers consistently recommend for hiking and camping, praising accessibility, scenic views, manageable difficulty, and peaceful atmosphere compared to busier peaks
